title: SK Hynix Pops 13% in Record $26.5bn Nasdaq Debut, Testing the AI Memory Trade
content: South Korean memory-chip maker and Nvidia supplier SK Hynix surged on its first day of US trading on Friday, opening around $170 — roughly 14% above its $149 offer price — and closing about 13% higher under the ticker SKHY. The company raised $26.5bn, the largest-ever US listing by a foreign company and the second-biggest US flotation on record after SpaceX four weeks earlier, and its strong reception helped the S&P 500 and Nasdaq secure weekly gains after a choppy stretch.
summary: SK Hynix closed roughly 13% above its offer price in a record $26.5bn Nasdaq debut, reinforcing appetite for the AI memory trade.
context:
• SK Hynix is the world's second-largest memory-chip maker and South Korea's second-most valuable company, a key supplier of high-bandwidth memory to Nvidia's AI accelerators.

• The listing rode a fierce 2026 rally in AI hardware, with memory prices firm amid persistent shortages and heavy data-centre demand.

• It follows SpaceX's US listing a month earlier, extending a run of mega-flotations that has revived a long-dormant IPO market.
happened:
• Shares opened near $170, about 14% above the $149 offer, and closed roughly 13% up on the session; demand for the sale had run at about seven times the shares on offer.

• The $26.5bn raised marked the biggest US listing ever by a foreign company and the second-largest overall after SpaceX.

• The S&P 500 rose 0.42% to 7,575.39, the Nasdaq added 0.29% to 26,281.61 and the Dow gained 0.29% to 52,637.01, with both the S&P and Nasdaq notching weekly wins.
implications:
• A successful debut hands US investors direct exposure to the memory-chip cycle and validates further Asian tech listings on Wall Street.

• The stock's aftermarket performance will be read as a live gauge of whether the AI capital-spending boom still has room to run.

• Heavy index-inclusion demand and scarcity of AI memory names could keep the shares volatile as lock-ups and follow-on supply are digested.
importance:
• The largest foreign US listing on record is a significant test of risk appetite at elevated equity valuations.

• As an Nvidia supplier, SK Hynix offers a cleaner read on AI memory demand than the broad chip indices.

• The debut links US, Korean and wider Asian equity sentiment to a single high-profile print.

title: Oil Jumps About 6% on the Week as US-Iran Fighting Chokes Hormuz
content: Crude posted a hefty weekly gain after renewed fighting between US and Iranian forces reignited fears over Gulf supply, with Brent trading around $76 a barrel and WTI near $72 on Friday. Iranian forces struck US military sites in Gulf states on Thursday following US attacks on Iran's coastal and eastern provinces, straining a three-week-old ceasefire and pushing traffic through the Strait of Hormuz — the route for about a fifth of the world's oil and gas — back to a near standstill.
summary: Brent rose about 6% on the week toward $76 as renewed US-Iran hostilities disrupted Strait of Hormuz shipping.
context:
• A three-week-old US-Iran ceasefire had begun to reopen Hormuz shipping before this week's escalation reversed much of that recovery.

• OPEC+ agreed on 5 July to lift output by a further 188,000 barrels a day from August, its fifth consecutive monthly increase, adding barrels into an uncertain market.

• The Strait of Hormuz carries roughly 20% of global oil and gas trade, leaving prices acutely sensitive to any disruption.
happened:
• Brent opened around $76.03 and WTI near $71.77 on Friday, leaving Brent up about 6% and WTI about 5% on the week.

• Iran attacked US bases in Gulf states on Thursday after US strikes on Iranian territory, and Hormuz transits fell back to a near standstill.

• The International Energy Agency warned that further escalation could upend an expected 2027 supply surplus, adding to the risk premium.
implications:
• A sustained Hormuz disruption would keep a geopolitical premium in crude and feed through to headline inflation and central-bank calculus.

• Hopes that Washington and Tehran return to diplomacy are capping the upside, leaving prices hostage to headlines.

• Additional OPEC+ barrels could cushion any pullback once tensions ease, but do little to offset an acute supply shock.
importance:
• Oil is the key transmission channel from Middle East geopolitics into global inflation and rates expectations.

• A renewed crude spike complicates the Federal Reserve's inflation fight just weeks before its next meeting.

• Energy is a swing factor for equity sectors, credit and emerging-market currencies alike.

title: Dollar Slides a Third Session as Iran Diplomacy Hopes Sap Haven Bid
content: The dollar extended its decline on Friday, with the dollar index easing toward 100.5 for a third straight losing session, as reports that Washington and Tehran would continue peace talks despite this week's flare-up dampened safe-haven demand for the currency. The move came even as a renewed oil-driven inflation scare nudged market-implied odds of a September Fed rate rise back up to about 62%.
summary: The dollar index slipped toward 100.5 for a third session as easing Middle East haven demand outweighed firmer Fed hike odds.
context:
• The greenback had drawn haven flows earlier in the week as US-Iran fighting escalated and crude prices surged.

• Under Fed Chair Kevin Warsh the policy rate has been held at 3.50%–3.75%, keeping the currency sensitive to shifting hike expectations.

• A soft June payrolls print of 57,000 had recently trimmed near-term tightening bets before the oil spike revived them.
happened:
• The dollar index fell toward 100.5, extending losses for a third consecutive session as diplomacy hopes cut the geopolitical risk premium.

• Market-implied odds of a September Fed hike firmed to roughly 62%, up from about 58% a week earlier but below the near-70% seen at mid-week.

• The currency's slide came despite higher oil prices, underlining how much the recent bid had rested on safe-haven flows.
implications:
• A softer dollar eases global financial conditions and offers relief to emerging-market currencies and dollar borrowers.

• Any fresh Middle East escalation could quickly reverse the move and restore haven demand.

• With hike odds finely balanced, each inflation and labour print now carries outsized currency risk.
importance:
• The dollar is the market's cleanest cross-asset read on shifting Fed expectations and global risk appetite.

• Currency swings of this scale ripple through commodities priced in dollars and cross-border capital flows.

• The tug-of-war between haven demand and hike bets frames positioning into the late-July Fed meeting.

title: Treasury Yields Steady Near 4.56% as Oil Spike Revives Hike Bets
content: The benchmark 10-year Treasury yield held around 4.56% on Friday as an oil-driven jump in inflation expectations offset the disinflationary read from a weak June jobs report. Traders lifted the implied probability of a September Federal Reserve rate rise back toward 60%, keeping front-end yields firm ahead of the 28–29 July policy meeting, where officials are expected to hold at 3.50%–3.75% while retaining the option to tighten.
summary: The 10-year yield held near 4.56% as a crude-led inflation scare pushed September Fed hike odds back toward 60% before the late-July meeting.
context:
• A soft June payrolls print of 57,000 had recently cut near-term hike bets, only for this week's oil surge to reverse the repricing.

• June FOMC projections showed a split committee, with several officials still pencilling in at least one further rate rise this year.

• Markets have swung sharply on each data point given how close the next policy call sits between a hold and a hike.
happened:
• The 10-year yield held around 4.56%, little changed on the day, as inflation and growth signals pulled in opposite directions.

• Implied odds of a September hike firmed back toward 60% after the renewed jump in crude prices.

• Attention turned to the 28–29 July FOMC, widely expected to leave rates unchanged while preserving a tightening bias.
implications:
• Sticky front-end yields raise borrowing costs across mortgages, corporate credit and emerging-market debt.

• A sustained crude rally could harden the hawkish case and lift yields further into the meeting.

• Renewed labour-market cooling would pull in the other direction, keeping rate volatility elevated.
importance:
• Treasury yields anchor the discount rate for every risk asset, from equities to housing.

• The two-way pull between oil-led inflation and a softening jobs market defines the current rates regime.

• Positioning into the late-July Fed decision hinges on how this balance resolves.

title: Gold Holds Above $4,100 as Inflation Fears Offset Firmer Rate Bets
content: Gold steadied above $4,100 an ounce on Friday and was on track to end a volatile week little changed, caught between oil-driven inflation fears that support bullion and firmer expectations for a Federal Reserve rate rise that weigh on it. Spot prices hovered near $4,100, with August futures around $4,135 an ounce, still well below January's record peak close to $5,600.
summary: Gold steadied above $4,100 an ounce for a broadly flat week as Middle East inflation fears offset firmer Fed hike expectations.
context:
• Gold hit a record near $5,600 in late January 2026 before retreating as the Fed signalled a higher-for-longer stance.

• Bullion typically benefits when real yields and the dollar fall, but suffers when rate-rise bets build.

• This year's Middle East conflict and steady central-bank buying have underpinned a firm floor under prices.
happened:
• Spot gold held above $4,100 an ounce, with August futures near $4,135, leaving the metal little changed on a choppy week.

• A roughly 6% weekly jump in oil after renewed US-Iran strikes lifted inflation fears and pushed September Fed hike odds toward 60%.

• Those firmer rate expectations capped bullion even as geopolitical risk provided safe-haven support.
implications:
• A crude-led inflation impulse cuts both ways for gold, supporting hedging demand while lifting the rate bets that pressure it.

• Continued official-sector buying may cushion pullbacks even if speculative demand cools.

• A clear break in either the oil price or Fed expectations would likely set gold's next directional move.
importance:
• Gold is a barometer of real rates, dollar strength and geopolitical risk appetite.

• Its direction shapes sentiment across the wider precious-metals and mining complex.

• A range-bound bullion price signals a market finely balanced between inflation and policy risks.

title: European Shares Tread Water Near Records as Tech Lags Hynix Debut
content: European equities ended the week broadly flat but close to record highs, with the pan-European Stoxx 600 edging up about 0.04% on Friday as technology shares lagged ahead of SK Hynix's blockbuster US listing. Italy's FTSE MIB rose 0.44% and London's FTSE 100 added 0.24%, while Germany's DAX slipped 0.2%, leaving the region consolidating a multi-week rally driven by rotation into cyclicals and defence names.
summary: The Stoxx 600 held near record highs, closing marginally higher as technology shares lagged before SK Hynix's US debut.
context:
• European indices have run to successive record highs in 2026, helped by easing US near-term rate-hike fears and a broadening rally.

• Gains have rotated beyond technology into cyclicals such as industrials and banks, and into defence shares amid heightened geopolitics.

• Regional bourses remain sensitive to Middle East headlines given their bearing on energy costs and inflation.
happened:
• The Stoxx 600 closed about 0.04% higher; the FTSE MIB rose 0.44%, the FTSE 100 gained 0.24% and the CAC 40 added 0.15%, while the DAX fell 0.2%.

• Technology shares opened around 0.8% lower, lagging the wider market as investors eyed SK Hynix's Nasdaq debut.

• The muted session left the index consolidating just below record highs after a strong recent run.
implications:
• A rally broadening beyond tech into cyclicals and defence suggests more durable breadth if it persists.

• Record valuations leave European stocks exposed to any renewed spike in energy prices or bond yields.

• Direction from here will lean on the coming US and European earnings seasons and Middle East developments.
importance:
• European benchmarks near records are a key gauge of global risk appetite outside the US.

• Sector rotation into defence and cyclicals signals shifting expectations for growth and geopolitics.

• The region's energy sensitivity makes it a barometer for the market impact of the Gulf conflict.

title: Wall Street Braces for Q2 Bank Earnings as JPMorgan Kicks Off Season
content: US investors are positioning for the start of second-quarter earnings season, with JPMorgan, Bank of America, Citigroup and Wells Fargo all due to report before the open on Tuesday 14 July. JPMorgan is forecast to post adjusted earnings of about $5.62 a share on roughly $49.5bn of revenue, and traders will treat the big lenders' results as an early read on credit quality, loan demand and the health of the wider economy.
summary: Major US banks report Q2 results on 14 July, kicking off earnings season and offering an early read on credit and the economy.
context:
• Bank earnings traditionally open the US reporting season and are treated as a cyclical bellwether for the broader economy.

• Analysts expect the finance sector to deliver Q2 earnings growth of about 12.5% on roughly 8.1% higher revenue.

• The reports land with equities near record highs and the Fed's rate path finely balanced.
happened:
• JPMorgan, Bank of America, Citigroup and Wells Fargo are all scheduled to report before the market open on Tuesday 14 July, with Goldman Sachs also in focus.

• JPMorgan's consensus adjusted EPS estimate of about $5.62 has been revised up nearly 4% over the past month.

• Investors flagged net interest margins, credit quality, commercial real estate and trading revenue as the key items to watch.
implications:
• Confident commentary on loan demand and credit would reassure on the economic backdrop and support the rally's breadth.

• Any deterioration in commercial real estate or consumer credit could reset expectations for the reporting season.

• Trading and investment-banking revenue will signal how firms fared through a volatile, geopolitically charged quarter.
importance:
• Bank results are an early, high-signal read on the economy that shapes expectations for the whole earnings season.

• Financials are a heavyweight index sector whose guidance moves the broader market.

• Credit-quality trends offer a forward look at where the cycle is heading.

title: Bitcoin ETF Flows Turn Positive After Record June Outflows
content: Bitcoin steadied around $64,000 and was up nearly 3% on the week as US spot exchange-traded funds drew about $510m over three consecutive sessions, ending a 10-day, $2.73bn outflow streak. The rebound, led by BlackRock's IBIT, followed a brutal June in which the funds shed a record $4.5bn — their worst month since launch — prompting Citigroup to cut its 12-month bitcoin target to $82,000 from $112,000.
summary: Bitcoin firmed near $64,000 as US spot ETFs pulled in about $510m over three sessions, reversing a record June of outflows.
context:
• Spot bitcoin ETF flows have become the dominant short-term driver of the token's price, with Citi research linking every $100m of net inflows to a same-day move of roughly half a percent.

• June's record $4.5bn of outflows pushed the funds' year-to-date flows negative for the first time since launch.

• Bitcoin has been trading in tandem with shifting Federal Reserve rate expectations ahead of the late-July meeting.
happened:
• US spot bitcoin ETFs took in about $510m over three straight sessions, ending a 10-day, $2.73bn outflow streak, with BlackRock's IBIT leading at $209m in one day.

• Bitcoin held around $64,000, up close to 3% on the week and roughly 10% higher so far in July.

• The turnaround followed a record June of outflows and Citi's cut to its 12-month target to $82,000.
implications:
• Sustained inflows led by a large institutional buyer would signal genuine re-entry rather than short-term dip-buying.

• Renewed outflows or a hawkish Fed surprise could quickly stall the recovery given the price's flow sensitivity.

• BlackRock's outsized share of assets makes the token increasingly hostage to a single issuer's flows.
importance:
• Bitcoin has become a closely watched gauge of speculative risk appetite and liquidity conditions.

• ETF flows now offer a transparent, high-frequency read on institutional demand for the asset.

• Swings of this scale spill over into the wider digital-asset and crypto-equity complex.

title: Chip Rally Broadens as Micron and AMD Soar, Nvidia Left Behind
content: The semiconductor complex has powered one of the market's biggest rallies of 2026, with the Philadelphia Semiconductor Index up more than 47% year-to-date as money floods into AI hardware. Yet the gains have been strikingly uneven: Micron has surged more than 300% and AMD around 170% this year, while sector bellwether Nvidia has limped to a gain of just over 3%, leaving traders to bet on a catch-up move in the AI leader.
summary: The Philadelphia Semiconductor Index is up over 47% in 2026 on an AI-driven surge, but Nvidia has badly lagged peers such as Micron and AMD.
context:
• Explosive AI infrastructure spending has driven a broad re-rating of chip stocks through 2026.

• Memory names have been among the biggest winners as data-centre demand and shortages lift prices.

• Intel's turnaround under Lip-Bu Tan has been aided by AI-server momentum and a $5bn equity investment from Nvidia.
happened:
• The Philadelphia Semiconductor Index has climbed more than 47% year-to-date, one of the sector's strongest runs in decades.

• Micron is up more than 300% and AMD around 170% this year, while Intel has surged sharply on its turnaround.

• Nvidia has gained only about 3% despite robust fundamentals, making it a conspicuous laggard as capital rotates into other chipmakers.
implications:
• The dispersion suggests investors are broadening AI exposure beyond the mega-cap leader into memory and second-tier names.

• If Nvidia's fundamentals hold, its underperformance could set up a catch-up trade that traders are already positioning for.

• A rally this concentrated in one sector raises the risk of a sharp reversal should AI-spending optimism fade.
importance:
• Semiconductors are the epicentre of the AI trade that has driven much of 2026's equity gains.

• Rotation within the sector is a key signal of how investors are pricing the durability of the AI boom.

• Nvidia's relative performance is a bellwether for sentiment across the entire complex.

title: Korean Chip Stocks Lead Asian Tech Higher Before Hynix Listing
content: Asian technology shares rallied to open the week, led by South Korea, as investors positioned ahead of SK Hynix's US listing and awaited Samsung's preliminary earnings. The Kospi rose about 2.7% on Monday, with Samsung Electronics up around 4% and SK Hynix higher, while Japan's Nikkei 225 added 0.7%, extending a rebound in the semiconductor trade across the region.
summary: South Korea's Kospi jumped about 2.7% as Samsung and SK Hynix led an Asian tech rally ahead of Hynix's US listing.
context:
• Korean chipmakers Samsung and SK Hynix sit at the heart of the global AI memory supply chain and dominate the Kospi.

• Investors were front-running index events, including SpaceX's addition to the Nasdaq 100, and SK Hynix's blockbuster US listing plan.

• A sharp run-up in the two heavyweights has fuelled debate over a possible bubble in Korean chip stocks.
happened:
• The Kospi rose about 2.71% on Monday, with Samsung Electronics up around 4% and SK Hynix also advancing.

• Japan's Nikkei 225 gained 0.73%, with the regional rally broadening across the semiconductor complex.

• Sentiment hinged on Samsung's upcoming preliminary earnings and anticipation of SK Hynix's US market debut.
implications:
• Continued strength in Korean chip names would reinforce the AI-hardware trade underpinning Asian equities.

• Elevated valuations leave the market exposed to any disappointment in Samsung's earnings or SK Hynix's US reception.

• The concentration of gains in two stocks heightens index-level risk if momentum stalls.
importance:
• Korean chipmakers are a direct read on global AI memory demand and the health of the wider chip cycle.

• The Kospi's swings illustrate how index events and mega-listings are driving Asian tech sentiment.

• Bubble concerns around the heavyweights carry implications for the broader regional benchmark.
